咨询对课题进展的update:
This meeting with Mr. Liu allowed me to decide the research topic and methodology. This study will adopt a case study approach to address the central question we established together: whether a drama-based educational intervention can effectively enhance understanding of gender relationships and self-protection knowledge among children in Yunnan. 
The first phase involves a preliminary investigation, as we agreed, to analyze contextual factors—including environmental, social, and economic dimensions—that shape local sex education landscapes. This analysis will directly inform the second phase, which centers on intervention delivery and data collection. As planned, an online drama program will be developed, with specific parameters such as knowledge of body autonomy and identification of inappropriate touch being defined. The mixed-methods approach will be operationalized through a pre-test/post-test model, using a quantitative questionnaire to measure cognitive changes and qualitative interviews to gather deeper narrative insights.
The final phase will focus on analysis. Quantitative data will be examined using statistical methods such as paired-sample t-tests to identify significant shifts, while qualitative data will be coded for thematic content.
